title: Jesus as the Son of God
intro: At the Jordan River, before Jesus had performed a single miracle or preached a single sermon, the Father spoke from heaven: this is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. His identity was declared before His ministry began. He was not the Son of God because of what He did. He did what He did because of who He is.
reading:
Matthew 3:17 establishes at the outset of Jesus' public ministry what is true from eternity — He is the beloved Son. John 1:34 records the testimony of John the Baptist: I have seen and have borne witness that this is the Son of God. This is not a title earned. It is an identity declared.

John 3:16-18 reveals the staggering implication of the Son's identity: God so loved the world that He gave His only Son. The giving of the Son is the measure of the Father's love. Whoever believes in Him is not condemned — because the Son was sent not to condemn the world but that the world might be saved through Him.

John 10:36 records Jesus claiming the title directly and without apology: I am the Son of God. Romans 1:3-4 tells us He was declared to be the Son of God in power by His resurrection from the dead. The resurrection was not when He became the Son — it was when that Sonship was vindicated before all creation.

1 John 4:15 draws the circle of communion: whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him and he in God. To confess the Son is to enter into relationship with the Father.

The identity of Jesus is not merely theology — it is the door through which we walk into the life of God.
reflection: When you confess that Jesus is the Son of God, does it feel like a creed you recite or a reality you inhabit? What would it mean to live from that confession today?
abide: Jesus, You are the Son of God — not by title but by nature, not by achievement but by being. I confess it today not as doctrine alone but as the foundation I stand on. You are who You say You are.
